summary: Syrian poet (born Ali Ahmad Said Esber) with a mythological pen name. Known for Songs of Mihyar the Damascene, 'A Grave for New York,' and the three-volume epic The Book (Al Kitab). Made the first complete Arabic translation of Ovid's Metamorphoses.

works.name: Songs of Mihyar the Damascene
works.description: Best-known collection; adopts the guise of a medieval Arab poet.

clues.clue: Best-known collection; adopts guise of medieval Arab poet Mihyar; contains prose 'Psalms'
examples: This author adopts the guise of a different medieval Arab poet in his best-known collection

clues.clue: Title character is 'neither a star, nor a prophet's inspiration' but 'a king whose dreams are palaces'
examples: 'neither a star, nor a prophet's inspiration' but 'a king whose dreams are palaces'

works.name: A Grave for New York
works.description: Poem addressing Walt Whitman and American themes.

clues.clue: Addresses Walt Whitman; Statue of Liberty holds up a rag called 'freedom' while 'choking a child named Earth'; title place equations
examples: described the Statue of Liberty holding up a rag called 'freedom' with one hand, and 'choking a child named Earth'

clues.clue: 'Up to now, Earth has been drawn as a pear / I mean breast'
examples: 'Up to now, Earth has been drawn as a pear / I mean breast'

works.name: The Book (Al Kitab)
works.description: Three-volume epic using the poet al-Mutannabi as a guide through the 6th-9th century Arab world.

clues.clue: Three-volume epic; speaker uses al-Mutannabi as guide through the 6th-9th century Arab world
examples: a three-volume epic whose speaker, employing the poet al-Mutannabi as a guide, travels throughout the sixth- to ninth-century Arab world

works.name: General
works.description: First complete Arabic translation of Metamorphoses; examines Surrealism and Sufism parallels.

clues.clue: First complete translation of Ovid's Metamorphoses into Arabic
examples: made the first complete translation of The Metamorphoses into Arabic

clues.clue: Book examining parallels between Surrealism and Sufism
examples: A book by this author examines the parallels between Surrealism and Sufism

comprehensive_summary: Adonis (born Ali Ahmad Said Esber, 1930) is a Syrian poet who adopted a mythological pen name. His best-known collection, Songs of Mihyar the Damascene, adopts the guise of a medieval Arab poet and contains prose 'Psalms.' His poem 'A Grave for New York' addresses Walt Whitman and describes the Statue of Liberty holding 'a rag called freedom' while 'choking a child named Earth.' The Book (Al Kitab) is a three-volume epic using al-Mutannabi as a guide through the Arab world. He made the first complete Arabic translation of Ovid's Metamorphoses and wrote a book examining parallels between Surrealism and Sufism.
